intro: Ocotea bissei, also known as Ocotea, is a species of tree native to the Andean regions of South America, belonging to the family Lauraceae. The Ocotea tree is valued for its health-promoting properties, including its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Bioactive constituents of Ocotea include sesquiterpenes, such as ocoteanol and ocotene, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ocotea is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or essential oil, which are used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
